_meta.purpose: Canonical hierarchy and quality requirements for each DCF input

forbidden.reason: Historical avg RF includes past inflation expectations not representative of current cost of capital
T-bill rate is distorted by monetary policy, not a fair long-term discount rate

ERP_equity_risk_premium.error_reference: ssrn-1025424 Error 1.B — using geometric vs arithmetic average can differ by 2–3%

forbidden.reason: Book D/E can be 5–10x market D/E, inflating levered beta significantly
Double-counts leverage cost — see R10 in model_selection_rules.json

Kd_cost_of_debt.error_reference: ssrn-1025424 Error 2.3 — using coupon rate instead of yield to maturity

FCF_free_cash_flow.definition: FCF = EBIT(1-T) + D&A - ΔNWC - CAPEX = OCF - CAPEX

forbidden.reason: Using non-normalized FCF as base overestimates all future projections

FCF_free_cash_flow.normalization: Use 2-year average OCF-CapEx to smooth one-time items
FCF_free_cash_flow.negative_fcf: Allowed in growth phase — do not force to zero. Source: ssrn-1025424 Error I.3.

WACC.definition: Discount rate that makes PV(FCF) == PV(ECF at Ke). NOT a cost. Source: ssrn-1620871.
WACC.values_for_E_D: VALUATION-DERIVED (iterative), not book or market cap. Source: ssrn-1620871 §3.

forbidden.reason: Real case: book-value WACC inflated equity from 2,014 to 3,033 — 51% overestimate. Source: ssrn-1620871.
Target ratio must match valuation-derived D/(D+E), not management aspiration

WACC.current_source: calculateWACC.ts — NOTE: uses market cap for weights (acceptable approximation)

TERMINAL_GROWTH.ceiling: Must be < WACC. Hard cap: WACC - 0.005. Source: ssrn-1025424 Error I.6.1.
TERMINAL_GROWTH.recommendation: Align with long-run nominal GDP growth (1.5–2.5%)
TERMINAL_GROWTH.terminal_value_weight_warning: Residual value = 60–80% of total value. Sensitivity analysis mandatory. Source: ssrn-1025424 §I.7.
TERMINAL_GROWTH.normalization_required: FCF at terminal year must be steady-state (no non-recurring items, consistent capex/D&A ratio). Source: ssrn-1025424 Error I.7.4.

SHARES_OUTSTANDING.use: Diluted shares outstanding (includes options, warrants, convertibles)

DEBT_VALUE.D_vs_N: Use market value (D) when bonds trade at significant discount/premium to par. For most public companies: D ≈ N (face value). Source: ssrn-1620871 §5.
DEBT_VALUE.financial_sector_special: For banks: use only long-term issued debt (bonds/notes), NOT deposit liabilities. Source: calculateWACC.ts.
